modules.heading: What's included in the system
modules.subtitle: Taska is not just a CRM. This is a single operating system that connects tasks, sales funnel, finance and business processes into one picture. Below are the modules that you will receive out of the box.
modules.subtitleHome: Eight modules in one contour — tap through to the page for depth.
modules.tasks.label: Tasks and projects
modules.tasks.title: Task Manager for the team
modules.tasks.desc: Manage tasks in one place: from incoming requests to internal department tasks. Kanban, table and Gantt - in one interface.
modules.tasks.b1: Kanban / table / Gantt for different teams
modules.tasks.b4: Task history and files in one window
modules.tasks.whoFor: Project managers, production, IT and operations teams: anyone who needs one point of truth on tasks and deadlines.
modules.tasks.f1: Kanban boards, table view and Gantt chart for different types of work - in one product.
modules.tasks.f2: Task templates and checklists for repeating processes (onboarding, release, purchasing).
modules.tasks.f3: Priorities, deadlines, performers, co-executors and observers with notifications.
modules.tasks.f4: Connection with the funnel and clients: task from the deal, comments and files without losing context.
modules.tasks.f5: Filters, tags, and project search to quickly find “what’s on.”
modules.tasks.f6: History of changes and attachments to the task - audit and transfer of cases between employees.
modules.tasks.sc1: An incoming application from the website turns into a task with a template: qualification, CP, call - with deadlines.
modules.tasks.sc2: Construction project: stages in Gantt, contractors see only their task packages.
modules.tasks.sc3: Weekly sprint: team board, WIP limits, report on closed tasks for the period.
modules.tasks.page.viewsTitle: Table, Kanban, and Gantt on one screen
modules.tasks.page.viewsLead: Switch to match the job: analytics and bulk actions in the table, flow on the board, deadlines and overlaps on the timeline.
modules.tasks.page.viewTableLead: Columns, filters, statuses, and priorities — when you need a view across the whole stream.
modules.tasks.page.viewKanbanLead: Status columns and cards — natural for production, support, and shift teams.
modules.tasks.page.viewGanttLead: Deadlines on a timeline: projects, phases, and dependencies in one line.
modules.tasks.page.detailLead: Status, priority, assignees, module, and dates next to description, attachments, and comments. Nothing gets lost in chat.
modules.tasks.page.featuresLead: Everything your team needs to stay in control without scattering across tools.
modules.tasks.page.scenariosLead: How the tasks module fits into real operations — from lead to reporting.
modules.tasks.page.moreModulesLead: Tasks connect to the funnel, clients, warehouse, and finance — explore the rest of the system.
modules.tasks.page.includedLead: What you get bundled with Tasks & projects.
modules.tasks.demo.row1: Business cards and shelf tags for the store
modules.tasks.demo.modalTitle: Business cards and shelf tags for the store
modules.tasks.demo.systemComment: Task created from the content plan
modules.tasks.demo.userComment: Approved with the client, waiting for mockups.
modules.funnel.label: Sales funnel
modules.funnel.title: CRM deal funnel
modules.funnel.desc: Keep track of all applications: from first contact to payment. The deal funnel is synchronized with tasks and reports.
modules.funnel.b1: Customizable funnel stages for your sales process
modules.funnel.b2: Deal card: contact, budget, source, history
modules.funnel.b3: Automatic creation of tasks by stages (call, meeting, CP)
modules.funnel.b4: Statistics on conversion and amount at each stage
modules.funnel.whoFor: Sales departments, account managers and commerce managers, where conversion, speed and revenue forecast are important.
modules.funnel.f1: Customizable funnel stages for your transaction cycle (B2B, services, large wholesale).
modules.funnel.f2: Deal card: contact, budget, lead source, competitors, next step.
modules.funnel.f3: Automatic creation of tasks when passing a stage: call, CP, demo, contract.
modules.funnel.f4: Probability and forecast along the funnel for cash planning and purchasing.
modules.funnel.f5: Funnel by sources and managers - where leads are lost and who attracts conversion.
modules.funnel.f6: History of transaction activity: letters, calls, meetings, files in one feed.
modules.funnel.sc1: The lead from the advertisement ends up in “New”; after qualification - in “Negotiations” with a task at the control point.
modules.funnel.sc2: A major transaction is divided into internal sub-stages of price negotiation and due diligence.
modules.funnel.sc3: An overdue transaction at the “Payment” stage is highlighted and creates a task for the financier and manager.
modules.funnel.page.viewsTitle: Kanban, list, and lost deals — one funnel
modules.funnel.page.viewsLead: Run deals the way your team likes: a stage board, a dense table, and a dedicated lost-deals view with sources.
modules.funnel.page.viewKanbanLead: Stage columns, amounts on cards, owner and lead source — the whole pipeline on one screen.
modules.funnel.page.viewListLead: A table across all deals: amount, stage, source, owner — great for reporting and exports.
modules.funnel.page.viewLostLead: Lost deals stay in history: reasons, amounts, and channels — so you can learn from the funnel.
modules.funnel.page.detailTitle: Deal, comms, and outcome in one place
modules.funnel.page.detailLead: Deal fields, stage, and amount on the left; chat, tasks, and meetings on the right — plus quick Win / Lost.
modules.funnel.page.builderLead: Build a funnel per business line: stage order, lead sources, and automatic tasks when a deal moves.
modules.funnel.page.featuresLead: Everything for transparent sales and forecasting without side spreadsheets.
modules.funnel.page.scenariosLead: How the funnel works together with tasks and reports.
modules.funnel.page.moreModulesLead: Deals connect to tasks, clients, and finance — assemble your operating loop.
modules.funnel.page.includedLead: What you get with the Sales funnel module on the platform.
modules.funnel.demo.chatEmpty: No messages on this deal yet
modules.clients.label: Clients and contracts
modules.clients.title: Client base and contracts
modules.clients.desc: All information about clients, legal entities and contracts is in one system. No more Excel and lost contacts.
modules.clients.b1: Customer cards with a history of all interactions
modules.clients.b2: Accounting for contracts, statuses and renewal terms
modules.clients.b3: Linking clients with transactions, tasks and payments
modules.clients.b4: Quick search by TIN, name and contacts
modules.clients.whoFor: Sales, legal department and back office: a single database of legal entities, contracts and contacts without duplication in Excel.
modules.clients.f1: Card of the organization and contact persons with roles (purchasing, director, accounting).
modules.clients.f2: Register of contracts: terms, amounts, auto-renewal, applications and files.
modules.clients.f3: The client’s connection with transactions, tasks and payments - a complete history of interactions.
modules.clients.f4: Search by tax ID, name, phone, and email for quick access.
modules.clients.f5: Segmentation and tags for marketing and call prioritization.
modules.clients.f6: Templates for documents and invoices with substitution of details from the card.
modules.clients.sc1: New counterparty: a card is created from a transaction; the contract is carried out in the same circuit.
modules.clients.sc2: An expiring contract provides a reminder to the person responsible and a task to extend or revise the terms.
modules.clients.sc3: Payment dispute: the manager opens the history of invoices and correspondence without a request to the accounting department.
modules.clients.page.viewsLead: From the client grid to the payment calendar and overdue list — without jumping between Excel and chats.
modules.clients.page.viewBaseLead: Cards with active contracts, services and amounts — add a new contract from the client in one click.
modules.clients.page.viewContractsLead: Table: client, amount, payment schedule, Active status — legal and commercial control in one place.
modules.clients.page.viewFinanceLead: Expected revenue, active contracts count and a payment calendar by day of the month.
modules.clients.page.viewDebtsLead: Overdue items with amount, due date and description — cash desk and managers see the same picture.
modules.clients.page.detailTitle: Company, notes and contracts in one window
modules.clients.page.detailLead: Details under Company, communication in Notes, contracts and amounts in a list — no duplicate data entry.
modules.clients.page.featuresLead: A single base for sales, legal and back office.
modules.clients.page.scenariosLead: How the module connects deals, tasks and payments.
modules.clients.page.moreModulesLead: Clients are linked to the funnel, tasks and finance — assemble the full loop.
modules.clients.page.includedLead: What the Clients and contracts module gives you.
modules.finance.label: Financial planning
modules.finance.title: P&L, funds, requests and reconciliation in one loop
modules.finance.desc: P&L by period, cash requests with CFO approval, bank statements and reconciliation with accounting — in one interface.
modules.finance.b1: P&L by month, quarter and year: income, expenses and profit by line
modules.finance.b2: Funds and period financial plan: revenue target and allocation to goals
modules.finance.b4: Statements, period KPIs and reconciliation with bank and ledger
modules.finance.whoFor: CFOs, finance directors, accounting and department heads who need one path from budget to actuals via statements.
modules.finance.f1: Financial planning sessions by department and period: draft vs posted, with CFO sign-off.
modules.finance.f2: P&L: line items, monthly and yearly views, clear profit picture.
modules.finance.f3: Financial plan: planned revenue (cash basis), allocation across funds — payroll, procurement, reserve and more.
modules.finance.f4: Spend requests: approval workflow; without the CFO a request does not enter financial planning.
modules.finance.f5: Statements and reconciliation: inflows, outflows, balance, fees; upload after planning and compare to the plan.
modules.finance.f6: Chart of accounts and funds (percent / fixed), exports for accounting; 1C and bank integration scoped per implementation.
modules.finance.sc1: An employee creates a request — the CFO approves or rejects; only approved items reach the planning session.
modules.finance.sc2: Planning is run and the plan is approved — a statement is uploaded and actual spend is checked against approved limits.
modules.finance.sc3: P&L is refreshed after month-end: variances by line, CFO notes and adjustments for the next period.
modules.finance.page.viewsLead: Planning, P&L, requests, statements and the financial plan — like the live product, without jumping between files.
modules.finance.page.viewPlanningLead: Cards by department and period: income, number of requests, “Created” status — then post and archive.
modules.finance.page.viewBdrLead: Income and expense lines, bottom-line profit: negatives in red, positives in green.
modules.finance.page.viewRequestsLead: Any cash request by line item; “Approved” and “Rejected” before amounts hit the plan.
modules.finance.page.viewStatementsLead: Inflow, outflow, period balance and fees; list of uploaded statements for ledger matching.
modules.finance.page.viewFinPlanLead: Department, period, revenue plan and fund split; save and post under CFO control.
modules.finance.page.cfoLead: The CFO is the gatekeeper: no request enters planning without them, and plans are not just saved — they are authorized.
modules.finance.page.cfo1: A spend request cannot enter a financial planning session without CFO approval.
modules.finance.page.cfo2: The CFO does not only save financial plans and sessions — they approve them, recording a management decision.
modules.finance.page.cfo3: After planning, statements are loaded: actual outflows are reconciled with approved limits and lines.
modules.finance.page.linksLead: Bank statement import and exchange with accounting (including 1C) is agreed during rollout for your bank and formats.
modules.finance.page.linkBankDesc: Import account movements, allocate to lines and reconcile with the period plan.
modules.finance.page.link1cDesc: Match bank and ledger turnover — one picture for CFO and accounting.
modules.finance.page.featuresLead: How the module covers budget-to-actual and who can authorize what.
modules.finance.page.includedLead: What the Taska finance core already includes.
modules.finance.page.scenariosLead: Typical flows: requests, planning, statement, reconciliation.
modules.finance.page.moreModulesLead: Finance links to the funnel, clients, warehouse and tasks — assemble an end-to-end loop.
modules.finance.demo.fpFundsHint: Fund amounts cannot exceed income for the period
modules.processes.label: Business processes
modules.processes.title: Templates, runs and steps to the finish line
modules.processes.desc: Design templates with steps and roles, launch instances and see the current step. Completed chains and tasks stay in one place — no chaos across chats and spreadsheets.
modules.processes.b2: Triggers: new order, status, date, amount
modules.processes.b4: Log of execution of each process step by step
modules.processes.whoFor: Process owners and operations directors who need to formalize “how we work” and remove chaos.
modules.processes.f1: Visual editor: blocks, conditions, branches without code.
modules.processes.f2: Triggers: new application, status change, date, amount threshold, login from Telegram.
modules.processes.f3: Automatic actions: create a task, send a message, change a stage, issue an invoice.
modules.processes.f4: Parallel and sequential branches, timeouts and escalations.
modules.processes.f5: Progress log: who completed the step and when, where it got stuck.
modules.processes.f6: Versioning and copying processes for new product lines.
modules.processes.sc1: New lead: automatic task to the manager, letter to the client, reminder in 24 hours.
modules.processes.sc2: If the transaction > N amount - additional approval from the director and a task for the lawyer.
modules.processes.sc3: Employee onboarding: checklist from HR, IT and access with deadlines by day.
modules.processes.page.viewsTitle: From template card to instance tasks
modules.processes.page.viewsLead: Tile and list views, versions, active and completed runs — like the product, plus the step and diagram builder.
modules.processes.page.viewTemplatesLead: Cards with step count, version and run counters; open and edit in a couple of clicks.
modules.processes.page.viewRunningLead: Current step, task progress and Active status; from the header — new template or start a process.
modules.processes.page.viewCompletedLead: Step timeline with completion marks, roles and assignees; instance task list with statuses.
modules.processes.page.viewEditorLead: Name, description, Steps and Diagram tabs: visual chain and save without developers.
modules.processes.page.editorLead: The same window as in the demo: draft, steps as a diagram and a save action.
modules.processes.page.featuresLead: What the processes module does together with tasks and the rest of the system.
modules.processes.page.includedLead: What the Business processes core includes.
modules.processes.page.moreModulesLead: Processes spawn tasks and connect to the funnel, finance and warehouse.
modules.processes.demo.runSectionLead: Active and paused instances. Completed ones are under Completed.
modules.processes.demo.compTasksHint: Click a task to open details
modules.processes.demo.editorVisHint: Process visualization. Edit steps and links on the Steps tab.
modules.analytics.label: Analytics and reports
modules.analytics.title: Dashboards and reports tailored to your company
modules.analytics.desc: We build analytics for specific clients: KPIs, funnel, finance, tasks and rankings in one workspace. Periods, reports and widgets follow your process—not a fixed template.
modules.analytics.b1: Sales funnel and revenue by period
modules.analytics.b2: Application processing speed and employee load
modules.analytics.b3: Reports on tasks, deadlines and statuses
modules.analytics.b4: Export to Google Sheets and PDF upon request
modules.analytics.whoFor: Owners and managers who need one dashboard for revenue, funnel, tasks and team.
modules.analytics.f1: Dashboards with KPIs: revenue, margin, conversion, transaction speed.
modules.analytics.f2: Sections by periods, departments, managers and lead sources.
modules.analytics.f3: Loading the team: tasks in progress, overdue, throughput.
modules.analytics.f4: Comparison of plan and fact for sales and production.
modules.analytics.f5: Export and schedule reports by e-mail to the manager.
modules.analytics.f6: Summary widgets for meetings: one screen - the whole picture.
modules.analytics.sc1: Weekly standup: a dashboard of the funnel and tasks for the past week is open.
modules.analytics.sc2: The owner looks at revenue by points and dynamics without a request to the analytics department.
modules.analytics.sc3: Quarterly review: comparison of KPIs of departments and adjustment of plans.
modules.analytics.page.bespokeLead: We shape dashboards, reports and slices together with you: by industry, team roles and the Taska modules you use. Each client gets their own screens and metrics.
modules.analytics.page.viewsTitle: Dashboard, statistics, and a report library
modules.analytics.page.viewsLead: Switch between KPI summary, detailed funnel and task stats, and ready-made reports—the same spirit as the live workspace.
modules.analytics.page.viewDashboardLead: Revenue, funnel, receivables, team and “Finance by period”—a snapshot for month, quarter or year.
modules.analytics.page.viewStatsLead: Funnel by stage, tasks by module and an employee performance table—for operational reviews.
modules.analytics.page.viewReportsLead: Finance, sales, HR and task log—report cards with descriptions; we agree content and formats with you.
modules.analytics.page.featuresLead: KPIs, slices, export and usage scenarios in Taska.
modules.analytics.page.includedLead: What the Analytics module gives when linked to the rest of the product.
modules.analytics.page.scenariosLead: How owners and managers use numbers without manual spreadsheets.
modules.analytics.page.moreModulesLead: Analytics builds on funnel, tasks, finance and team—connect the blocks you need for one coherent picture.
modules.analytics.demo.financeSectionLea: Checking account (statements) and the Finance module—executed planning and plan documents. Period: current month.
modules.team.label: Employees and organizational structure
modules.team.title: Cards, org chart, and company structure
modules.team.desc: Keep profiles with contacts and dates, see hierarchy on the org chart, and assign people to roles in the structure—including “unassigned” until placement. One module for HR, managers, and admins.
modules.team.b1: Organizational structure of the company and roles in the team
modules.team.b2: Access rights by departments and modules
modules.team.b3: Employee work history in tasks and transactions
modules.team.b4: Transparency of responsibility for each process
modules.team.whoFor: HR, managers and IT administrators: who is in what role, what data has access to, how the organizational structure is structured.
modules.team.f1: Organizational structure: divisions, positions, direct managers.
modules.team.f2: Roles and rights: access to modules, transactions, finances according to company policy.
modules.team.f3: Assigning responsibility for tasks and transactions, taking into account substitutions.
modules.team.f4: Employee profile: contacts, participation in projects and activity history.
modules.team.f5: Onboarding: checklist for issuing access upon admission and closing upon dismissal.
modules.team.f6: Audit: who changed critical settings and rights.
modules.team.sc1: New employee: added to the organizational chart, given the role of “Sales Manager” with visibility only to his own transactions.
modules.team.sc2: Transfer to another department: change of department and automatic revision of access.
modules.team.sc3: Dismissal: blocking entry, transferring tasks to another performer according to a template.
modules.team.page.viewsTitle: Cards, tree, and roles by department
modules.team.page.viewsLead: Switch between a profile grid, a visual org chart, and structure with unassigned employees—like the real workspace.
modules.team.page.viewCardsLead: Position, hire date, birthday, phone, and Telegram—quick access to contacts and editing.
modules.team.page.viewOrgLead: Positions, departments, and people on nodes: from CEO to line roles and reporting lines.
modules.team.page.viewStructLead: Unassigned employees are separate; under each role—assigned people. One person can hold several positions.
modules.team.page.featuresLead: Org structure, rights, profiles, and typical HR scenarios in Taska.
modules.team.page.includedLead: What the Employees module already gives in the core.
modules.team.page.scenariosLead: Hiring, transfer, and dismissal without losing control over access.
modules.team.page.moreModulesLead: Team links to tasks, funnel, finance, and processes—assemble the full loop.
modules.warehouse.label: Warehouse and remains
modules.warehouse.title: Warehouse accounting and movement of goods
modules.warehouse.desc: Balances at warehouses and points, receipts and expenses, movements and inventory - in conjunction with sales and purchases, without separate tables.
modules.warehouse.b1: Remains by product range, warehouses and retail outlets
modules.warehouse.b2: Arrival, shipment, movements and write-offs with history
modules.warehouse.b3: Reservation for orders and control of minimum stocks
modules.warehouse.b4: Connection with funnel, purchasing and finance
modules.warehouse.whoFor: Operations directors, warehouse, purchasing and retail: when you need to see real balances and not lose movement between points.
modules.warehouse.f1: Nomenclature card: photos, description, multiple barcodes (EAN/GTIN), QR for fast lookup on the floor and in retail.
modules.warehouse.f2: Batches and shelf life, serial tracking for selected categories, bins and storage zones — mature WMS practices.
modules.warehouse.f3: Multi-warehouse: main, retail, transit; transfers between locations with full history.
modules.warehouse.f4: Operations journal: inbound, shipment, write-off, adjustment — no “lost” movements or double accounting.
modules.warehouse.f5: Audits and inventory: book vs actual, discrepancy act and stock correction in one loop.
modules.warehouse.f6: Turnover, slow movers, critical stock alerts and notifications; exports for procurement and finance.
modules.warehouse.sc1: The point ordered a replenishment - movement from the central warehouse with a receiving task and quantity reconciliation.
modules.warehouse.sc2: The customer places an order - the goods are reserved, shipment creates a task for the collector and courier.
modules.warehouse.sc3: Planned inventory: checklists by zone, discrepancies go to reconciliation and accounting adjustments.
modules.warehouse.page.viewsKicker: Four tabs — like the live demo
modules.warehouse.page.viewsLead: From stock KPIs to book-vs-actual audits — one interface instead of spreadsheets and chats.
modules.warehouse.page.viewBalancesLead: Stock value, SKUs below minimum, and statuses: OK, low stock, reserved for an order.
modules.warehouse.page.viewNomenclatureL: Photo and description, category, batch, barcode and QR — for picking, receiving and storefronts.
modules.warehouse.page.viewJournalLead: Receipt, adjustment, transfer between warehouses — with date, comment and clear operation types.
modules.warehouse.page.viewRevisionsLead: Book and actual quantities per line: discrepancies are visible immediately and flow into correction.
modules.warehouse.page.integrationsLead: Sync stock and catalog with your online store and platforms — priorities and timelines agreed during rollout.
modules.warehouse.page.int1Desc: Export stock and availability for the catalog so you do not sell air.
modules.warehouse.page.int2Desc: Seller-model stock and order exchange — we connect as your SKU count grows.
modules.warehouse.page.int3Desc: Unified SKUs and warehouses for e-commerce without double books.
modules.warehouse.page.int4Desc: Webhooks and file exchange with a partner when part of the stock is outsourced.
modules.warehouse.page.featuresLead: Below is where the module is heading: from core stock control toward strong WMS and omnichannel patterns.
modules.warehouse.page.includedLead: What the Warehouse core in Taska already covers.
modules.warehouse.page.scenariosLead: How warehouse connects to sales, tasks and finance.
modules.warehouse.page.moreModulesLead: Warehouse links to the funnel, finance and tasks — assemble an end-to-end loop.
modules.warehouse.demo.nomCard1Desc: Medium roast, chocolate and citrus notes. Store in a dry place.
modules.warehouse.demo.nomCard2Desc: Kraft with window, for bakery and takeaway. Photo for storefront and marketplaces.
modules.custom.heading: Customization for your business
modules.custom.title: We customize modules for your processes
modules.custom.desc: Basic Taska already covers typical tasks for tasks, sales and finance. Next, we adapt the system to your industry: add fields, statuses, automation and reports. In the end you get managed business, not “another CRM”.
